title: Carbon sinks oceans
script_summary: Discover how oceans act as critical carbon sinks and why it matters for our planet.

segments.voiceover: Science has a clearer story about carbon sinks oceans than social media does.
Let’s simplify: carbon sinks are natural systems that absorb carbon dioxide.
Oceans are the largest carbon sinks on Earth, capturing about a quarter of our CO2 emissions.
Through photosynthesis, marine plants and phytoplankton play a vital role in this process.
But, rising temperatures and pollution threaten these essential ecosystems.
What if I told you that protecting oceans is key to fighting climate change?
Learn more about how you can help preserve these carbon sinks. Follow for more insights!
